The piezoelectric effect involves two main directions of conversion:

1.  **Voltage-to-mechanical vibration**: When an electric voltage is applied to a piezoelectric material, it causes the material to vibrate mechanically. This mechanical vibration is then used to generate sound waves in ultrasound imaging.
2.  **Mechanical vibration-to-voltage**: Conversely, when a piezoelectric material vibrates mechanically due to sound waves, it generates an electric voltage. In ultrasound imaging, this generated voltage is converted back into an electrical signal that can be processed and visualized as an image.

These two directions of conversion are fundamental to the operation of piezoelectric transducers in ultrasound imaging, enabling both the generation of high-frequency sound waves for imaging and the detection of returning echoes.
